title: Tiffin
subtitle: Fridge-set chocolate biscuit traybake
excerpt: A no-bake traybake of butter, golden syrup, cocoa, broken digestives, dried fruit, and dark chocolate. Fridge-set, sliced cold. Simple and very reliable.
sourceNotes: Tiffin and chocolate biscuit cake are the same bake by different names. The recipe is documented in British cookery columns throughout the mid-twentieth century. The technique (melting butter, syrup, and cocoa then binding broken biscuits) appears in domestic encyclopaedias from the 1940s onwards.

recipe.freezeNotes: Wrap individual pieces in cling film. Freeze in a bag for up to 2 months. Thaw in the fridge.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps in the fridge for up to a week. The flavour deepens on day two.

glossaryTerms.definition: A process of melting and then cooling chocolate through specific temperature stages so that the cocoa butter re-forms stable crystals. Tempered chocolate sets with a glossy finish and a clean snap. For tiffin, tempering is optional — the topping simply needs to be melted and poured on, though it may bloom slightly.

content.text: Tiffin requires no oven and almost no technique. The skill, such as it is, is in the ratio: enough fat and syrup to bind the broken biscuits firmly, not so much that it becomes greasy. The dried fruit adds chew; the salt anchors the sweetness. The topping is a straight melt of dark chocolate, no
needed unless you want the professional snap and gloss.
Line a 20×20 cm tin with baking paper, leaving an overhang on two sides. No need to grease.

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}}, {{golden-syrup}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} together in a medium saucepan over low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and add the broken {{digestive-biscuits}} and {{raisins}}. Stir until everything is evenly coated. Tip into the tin and press firmly into an even layer. Refrigerate for 30 minutes while you melt the chocolate.

Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, stirring until smooth. Do not let the bowl touch the water. Pour over the set base and spread to an even layer with a palette knife.

Return to the fridge for at least 90 minutes, until the chocolate is completely set. Lift out by the paper, place on a board, and cut into 16 pieces with a sharp knife. For clean cuts, warm the blade briefly under hot water and dry it between cuts.
